coverage.notes: Coverage tool does not instrument source-generator partial classes. Tests DO exercise the code (verified by tracing). Only Engine/this.cs (84.8%) appears in coverage.

findings.issue: Engine.Providers Get<T>(), Has<T>(), Remove<T>() have zero tests — only Register and GetOrDefault are exercised
findings.impact: A bug in Remove<T>() or Has<T>() (e.g., wrong type key) would go undetected
findings.suggestion: Add a dedicated ProviderRegistryTests class testing all 5 public methods

findings.issue: Hash_ObjectInput_SerializesToJsonBeforeHashing claims JSON serialization but only checks consistency (same hash twice), not that JSON serialization actually happened
findings.impact: If SerializeData switched from JsonSerializer to ToString(), test would still pass
findings.suggestion: Add a known-value test: hash of {"Name":"test","Value":42} with keccak256 should equal a pre-computed hex string

findings.issue: Hash_ExplicitAlgorithm_OverridesDefault checks algorithm name and hash length but not that the hash value differs from default (keccak256)
findings.impact: If Algorithm parameter were ignored, test would still pass — both algorithms produce 64-char hex
findings.suggestion: Hash same input with keccak256 and sha256, assert hashes differ

findings.issue: 6 PLang .goal tests exist but cannot be built or run — crypto module not registered with builder yet
findings.impact: Full pipeline validation (goal → builder → .pr → runtime) deferred until builder integration (piece 8)
findings.suggestion: Build and run PLang tests after crypto module is registered with the builder

findings.impact: Minimal — convenience method returning Hash property

summary: Test suite is honest and well-structured. All critical paths covered with strong assertions (known test vectors, error keys, status codes). 5 minor findings — no false greens, no missing critical coverage. Provider error propagation (review-driven fix from codeanalyzer v1) is properly tested. PLang tests deferred until builder integration.
